scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL JUDGE, BENGALURU

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

BETWEEN:

Ram Kumar,
S/o {{PLACEHOLDER_FATHER'S_NAME}},
Aged about 45 years,
R/o Jayanagar, Bengaluru,
Karnataka – {{PLACEHOLDER_PIN}}.      ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
S/o {{PLACEHOLDER_FATHER'S_NAME}},
Aged about 38 years,
R/o Koramangala, Bengaluru,
Karnataka – {{PLACEHOLDER_PIN}}.      ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

The Plaintiff above named most respectfully submits as under:

FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, and is a law-abiding citizen. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, and is known to the Plaintiff.

2. On or about 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant. The said amount was transferred via NEFT bank transfer from the Plaintiff's bank account to the Defendant's bank account. The Unique Transaction Reference (UTR) number for the said NEFT transfer is AXIB20240315123456.

3. There was no written agreement executed between the parties regarding the said loan. The transaction was based on the personal relationship and oral understanding between the parties.

4. The Defendant acknowledged the loan through WhatsApp messages sent to the Plaintiff, wherein the Defendant confirmed receipt of the loan amount and promised to repay the same.

5. Despite repeated oral demands by the Plaintiff, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the loan amount. The Plaintiff made several oral requests for repayment, but the Defendant has been evading the same.

6. The date of default is 20.06.2024, from which date the Defendant has failed to repay the principal amount despite demands.

7. The Plaintiff caused a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to be issued to the Defendant, calling upon the Defendant to repay the loan amount along with interest. Despite receipt of the legal notice, the Defendant has failed to comply with the same.

8. The Defendant is liable to pay interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000 from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, until realization.

9. This Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit as the parties reside within the jurisdiction of this Court and the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court.

10. The suit is valued for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) plus interest, and court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid thereon.

STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT

The Statement of Account showing the amount due and payable by the Defendant to the Plaintiff is as under:

| S. No. | Particulars | Amount (Rs.) |
|--------|-------------|--------------|
| 1. | Principal amount advanced on 15.03.2024 | 15,00,000 |
| 2. | Part-payments received | Nil |
| 3. | Balance Principal due | 15,00,000 |
| 4. | Interest @ 12% p.a. from 20.06.2024 to date of filing | {{PLACEHOLDER_INTEREST_AMOUNT}} |
| | TOTAL AMOUNT DUE | {{PLACEHOLDER_TOTAL_AMOUNT}} |

CAUSE OF ACTION

11. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024, when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite the same being due and payable, and despite oral demands by the Plaintiff.

12. The cause of action further arose on 01.07.2024, when the Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant calling upon the Defendant to repay the loan amount, and the Defendant failed to comply with the said notice.

13. The cause of action is continuing within the jurisdiction of this Court, and the suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The Plaintiff relies on Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides for compensation for loss or damage caused by breach of contract. The Defendant's failure to repay the loan amount constitutes a breach of the oral contract, entitling the Plaintiff to recover the principal amount along with interest.

15. The Plaintiff relies on Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which confers jurisdiction on Civil Courts to try all suits of a civil nature except those expressly barred. The present suit for recovery of money is a civil suit triable by this Court.

16. The Plaintiff relies on Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which provides that every suit shall be instituted in a Court within the local limits of whose jurisdiction the Defendant resides or carries on business, or where the cause of action arises. The Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Court, and the cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of this Court.

17. The Plaintiff relies on Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which empowers the Court to award interest at such rate as the Court deems reasonable from the date of decree until realization. The Plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default until realization.

18. The suit is withi
